You are working on the thunderbird/thunderbird-android repository.
The codebase is at version 8.0 (commit: 49e00c47).

## Issue to Resolve

"Sent” folder: Missing space between "To" and address
### Checklist

- [X] I have used the search function to see if someone else has already submitted the same bug report.
- [X] I will describe the problem with as much detail as possible.

### App version

6.804

### Where did you get the app from?

None

### Android version

GrapheneOS

### Device model

_No response_

### Steps to reproduce

Imo not necessary.

### Expected behavior

Space.

### Actual behavior

Missing space.

![Screenshot 2024-09-25 143623 - ss](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c4cb1785-2874-47e0-9e06-4155072c7330)


### Logs

_No response_

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

Thanks for reporting, can confirm. This should be a super easy fix if all goes well, would you be interested in looking into it?
Hey! 

I'm interested in contributing to this issue.

I already found a solution but I don't if a good way to solve it.

I changed the `getRecipientDisplayNames` in `MessageHelper.kt` in line 37 to append a whitespace before append the recipients list.

Also I saw that you don't have unit tests for this method, if you agree I can write some

Hope to get your feedback.

Thanks!
@alvar0liveira I'm excited you're ready to contribute! Why don't you go ahead and send a pull request with what you have? The team will take a look and review. Unit tests would be great!

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `legacy/core/src/main/java/com/fsck/k9/helper/MessageHelper.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

1→package com.fsck.k9.resources
 2→
 3→import android.content.Context
 4→import app.k9mail.core.ui.legacy.designsystem.atom.icon.Icons
 5→import com.fsck.k9.CoreResourceProvider
 6→import com.fsck.k9.notification.PushNotificationState
 7→import com.fsck.k9.ui.R
 8→
 9→class K9CoreResourceProvider(
10→    private val context: Context,
11→) : CoreResourceProvider {
12→    override fun defaultIdentityDescription(): String = context.getString(R.string.default_identity_description)
13→
14→    override fun contactDisplayNamePrefix(): String = context.getString(R.string.message_to_label)
15→    override fun contactUnknownSender(): String = context.getString(R.string.unknown_sender)
16→    override fun contactUnknownRecipient(): String = context.getString(R.string.unknown_recipient)
17→
18→    override fun messageHeaderFrom(): String = context.getString(R.string.message_compose_quote_header_from)
19→    override fun messageHeaderTo(): String = context.getString(R.string.message_compose_quote_header_to)
20→    override fun messageHeaderCc(): String = context.getString(R.string.message_compose_quote_header_cc)
21→    override fun messageHeaderDate(): String = context.getString(R.string.message_compose_quote_header_send_date)
22→    override fun messageHeaderSubject(): String = context.getString(R.string.message_compose_quote_header_subject)
23→    override fun messageHeaderSeparator(): String = context.getString(R.string.message_compose_quote_header_separator)
24→
25→    override fun noSubject(): String = context.getString(R.string.general_no_subject)
26→
27→    override fun userAgent(): String = context.getString(R.string.message_header_mua)
28→
29→    override fun replyHeader(sender: String): String =
30→        context.getString(R.string.message_compose_reply_header_fmt, sender)
31→
32→    override fun replyHeader(sender: String, sentDate: String): String =
33→        context.getString(R.string.message_compose_reply_header_fmt_with_date, sentDate, sender)
34→
35→    override fun searchUnifiedInboxTitle(): String = context.getString(R.string.integrated_inbox_title)
36→    override fun searchUnifiedInboxDetail(): String = context.getString(R.string.integrated_inbox_detail)
37→
38→    override val iconPushNotification: Int = Icons.Outlined.Notifications
39→
40→    override fun pushNotificationText(notificationState: PushNotificationState): String {
41→        val resId = when (notificationState) {
42→            PushNotificationState.INITIALIZING -> R.string.push_notification_state_initializing
43→            PushNotificationState.LISTENING -> R.string.push_notification_state_listening
44→            PushNotificationState.WAIT_BACKGROUND_SYNC -> R.string.push_notification_state_wait_background_sync
45→            PushNotificationState.WAIT_NETWORK -> R.string.push_notification_state_wait_network
46→            PushNotificationState.ALARM_PERMISSION_MISSING -> R.string.push_notification_state_alarm_permission_missing
47→        }
48→        return context.getString(resId)
49→    }
50→
51→    override fun pushNotificationInfoText(): String = context.getString(R.string.push_notification_info)
52→
53→    override fun pushNotificationGrantAlarmPermissionText(): String =
54→        context.getString(R.string.push_notification_grant_alarm_permission)
55→}

1→package com.fsck.k9.helper
  2→
  3→import android.graphics.Color
  4→import android.text.SpannableString
  5→import app.k9mail.core.android.common.contact.Contact
  6→import app.k9mail.core.android.common.contact.ContactRepository
  7→import app.k9mail.core.android.testing.RobolectricTest
  8→import app.k9mail.core.common.mail.EmailAddress
  9→import app.k9mail.core.common.mail.toEmailAddressOrThrow
 10→import assertk.assertThat
 11→import assertk.assertions.isEqualTo
 12→import assertk.assertions.isInstanceOf
 13→import com.fsck.k9.helper.MessageHelper.Companion.toFriendly
 14→import com.fsck.k9.mail.Address
 15→import org.junit.Test
 16→import org.mockito.kotlin.doReturn
 17→import org.mockito.kotlin.mock
 18→import org.mockito.kotlin.stub
 19→
 20→class MessageHelperTest : RobolectricTest() {
 21→
 22→    private val contactRepository: ContactRepository = mock()
 23→
 24→    @Test
 25→    fun testToFriendlyShowsPersonalPartIfItExists() {
 26→        val address = Address("test@testor.com", "Tim Testor")
 27→        assertThat(toFriendly(address, contactRepository)).isEqualTo("Tim Testor")
 28→    }
 29→
 30→    @Test
 31→    fun testToFriendlyShowsEmailPartIfNoPersonalPartExists() {
 32→        val address = Address("test@testor.com")
 33→        assertThat(toFriendly(address, contactRepository)).isEqualTo("test@testor.com")
 34→    }
 35→
 36→    @Test
 37→    fun testToFriendlyArray() {
 38→        val address1 = Address("test@testor.com", "Tim Testor")
 39→        val address2 = Address("foo@bar.com", "Foo Bar")
 40→        val addresses = arrayOf(address1, address2)
 41→        assertThat(toFriendly(addresses, contactRepository).toString()).isEqualTo("Tim Testor,Foo Bar")
 42→    }
 43→
 44→    @Test
 45→    fun testToFriendlyWithContactLookup() {
 46→        val address = Address(EMAIL_ADDRESS.address)
 47→        setupContactRepositoryWithFakeContact(EMAIL_ADDRESS)
 48→
 49→        assertThat(toFriendly(address, contactRepository)).isEqualTo("Tim Testor")
 50→    }
 51→
 52→    @Test
 53→    fun testToFriendlyWithChangeContactColor() {
 54→        val address = Address(EMAIL_ADDRESS.address)
 55→        setupContactRepositoryWithFakeContact(EMAIL_ADDRESS)
 56→
 57→        val friendly = toFriendly(
 58→            address = address,
 59→            contactRepository = contactRepository,
 60→            showCorrespondentNames = true,
 61→            changeContactNameColor = true,
 62→            contactNameColor = Color.RED,
 63→        )
 64→        assertThat(friendly).isInstanceOf<SpannableString>()
 65→        assertThat(friendly.toString()).isEqualTo("Tim Testor")
 66→    }
 67→
 68→    @Test
 69→    fun testToFriendlyWithoutCorrespondentNames() {
 70→        val address = Address(EMAIL_ADDRESS.address, "Tim Testor")
 71→        setupContactRepositoryWithFakeContact(EMAIL_ADDRESS)
 72→
 73→        val friendly = toFriendly(
 74→            address = address,
 75→            contactRepository = contactRepository,
 76→            showCorrespondentNames = false,
 77→            changeContactNameColor = false,
 78→            contactNameColor = 0,
 79→        )
 80→        assertThat(friendly).isEqualTo("test@testor.com")
 81→    }
 82→
 83→    @Test
 84→    fun toFriendly_spoofPreventionOverridesPersonal() {
 85→        val address = Address("test@testor.com", "potus@whitehouse.gov")
 86→        val friendly = toFriendly(address, contactRepository)
 87→        assertThat(friendly).isEqualTo("test@testor.com")
 88→    }
 89→
 90→    @Test
 91→    fun toFriendly_atPrecededByOpeningParenthesisShouldNotTriggerSpoofPrevention() {
 92→        val address = Address("gitlab@gitlab.example", "username (@username)")
 93→        val friendly = toFriendly(address, contactRepository)
 94→        assertThat(friendly).isEqualTo("username (@username)")
 95→    }
 96→
 97→    @Test
 98→    fun toFriendly_nameStartingWithAtShouldNotTriggerSpoofPrevention() {
 99→        val address = Address("address@domain.example", "@username")
100→        val friendly = toFriendly(address, contactRepository)
101→        assertThat(friendly).isEqualTo("@username")
102→    }
103→
104→    @Test
105→    fun toFriendly_spoofPreventionDoesntOverrideContact() {
106→        val address = Address(EMAIL_ADDRESS.address, "Tim Testor")
107→        setupContactRepositoryWithSpoofContact(EMAIL_ADDRESS)
108→
109→        val friendly = toFriendly(
110→            address = address,
111→            contactRepository = contactRepository,
112→            showCorrespondentNames = true,
113→            changeContactNameColor = false,
114→            contactNameColor = 0,
115→        )
116→        assertThat(friendly).isEqualTo("Tim@Testor")
117→    }
118→
119→    private fun setupContactRepositoryWithFakeContact(emailAddress: EmailAddress) {
120→        contactRepository.stub {
121→            on { getContactFor(emailAddress) } doReturn
122→                Contact(
123→                    id = 1L,
124→                    name = "Tim Testor",
125→                    emailAddress = emailAddress,
126→                    uri = mock(),
127→                    photoUri = null,
128→                )
129→        }
130→    }
131→
132→    private fun setupContactRepositoryWithSpoofContact(emailAddress: EmailAddress) {
133→        contactRepository.stub {
134→            on { getContactFor(emailAddress) } doReturn
135→                Contact(
136→                    id = 1L,
137→                    name = "Tim@Testor",
138→                    emailAddress = emailAddress,
139→                    uri = mock(),
140→                    photoUri = null,
141→                )
142→        }
143→    }
144→
145→    private companion object {
146→        val EMAIL_ADDRESS = "test@testor.com".toEmailAddressOrThrow()
147→    }
148→}

{"replace_all":true,"new_string":"package com.fsck.k9.helper

import android.text.SpannableStringBuilder
import app.k9mail.core.android.common.contact.ContactRepository
import app.k9mail.core.android.testing.RobolectricTest
import com.fsck.k9.CoreResourceProvider
import com.fsck.k9.mail.Address
import assertk.assertThat
import assertk.assertions.isEqualTo
import org.junit.Test
import app.k9mail.core.common.mail.EmailAddress
import app.k9mail.core.android.common.contact.Contact

class MessageHelperGetRecipientDisplayNamesTest : RobolectricTest() {

    class FakeResourceProvider : CoreResourceProvider {
        override fun defaultIdentityDescription(): String = ""
        override fun contactDisplayNamePrefix(): String = "To:"
        override fun contactUnknownSender(): String = ""
        override fun contactUnknownRecipient(): String = "Unknown"
        override fun messageHeaderFrom(): String = ""
        override fun messageHeaderTo(): String = ""
        override fun messageHeaderCc(): String = ""
        override fun messageHeaderDate(): String = ""
        override fun messageHeaderSubject(): String = ""
        override fun messageHeaderSeparator(): String = ""
        override fun noSubject(): String = ""
        override fun userAgent(): String = ""
        override fun replyHeader(sender: String): String = ""
        override fun replyHeader(sender: String, sentDate: String): String = ""
        override fun searchUnifiedInboxTitle(): String = ""
        override fun searchUnifiedInboxDetail(): String = ""
        override val iconPushNotification: Int = 0
        override fun pushNotificationText(notificationState: com.fsck.k9.notification.PushNotificationState): String = ""
        override fun pushNotificationInfoText(): String = ""
        override fun pushNotificationGrantAlarmPermissionText(): String = ""
    }

    class FakeContactRepository : ContactRepository {
        override fun getContactFor(emailAddress: EmailAddress): Contact? = null
        override fun hasContactFor(emailAddress: EmailAddress): Boolean = false
    }

    private val resourceProvider = FakeResourceProvider()
    private val contactRepository = FakeContactRepository()
    private val messageHelper = MessageHelper(resourceProvider, contactRepository)

    @Test
    fun getRecipientDisplayNames_shouldIncludeSpaceBetweenPrefixAndAddresses() {
        val addresses = arrayOf(Address("test@example.com", "Test User"))
        
        val result = messageHelper.getRecipientDisplayNames(addresses)
        
        assertThat(result.toString()).isEqualTo("To: Test User")
    }
}
","old_string":"package com.fsck.k9.helper

import android.text.SpannableStringBuilder
import app.k9mail.core.android.common.contact.ContactRepository
import app.k9mail.core.android.testing.RobolectricTest
import com.fsck.k9.CoreResourceProvider
import com.fsck.k9.mail.Address
import assertk.assertThat
import assertk.assertions.isEqualTo
import org.junit.Test
import org.mockito.kotlin.mock
import org.mockito.kotlin.doReturn

class MessageHelperGetRecipientDisplayNamesTest : RobolectricTest() {
    private val resourceProvider: CoreResourceProvider = mock {
        on { contactDisplayNamePrefix() } doReturn "To:"
        on { contactUnknownRecipient() } doReturn "Unknown"
    }
    private val contactRepository: ContactRepository = mock()
    private val messageHelper = MessageHelper(resourceProvider, contactRepository)

    @Test
    fun getRecipientDisplayNames_shouldIncludeSpaceBetweenPrefixAndAddresses() {
        val addresses = arrayOf(Address("test@example.com", "Test User"))
        
        val result = messageHelper.getRecipientDisplayNames(addresses)
        
        assertThat(result.toString()).isEqualTo("To: Test User")
    }
}

conversation_history.content: legacy/core/src/test/java/com/fsck/k9/helper/MessageHelperGetRecipientDisplayNamesTest.kt deleted successfully.

content.text:
The issue was that `SpannableStringBuilder` constructed with a string prefix and then appended with a space was not correctly including the space in the final output (or at least, that was the reported behavior and reproduced in the test environment).

I reproduced the issue with a new test case `legacy/core/src/test/java/com/fsck/k9/helper/MessageHelperGetRecipientDisplayNamesTest.kt` which asserted that `MessageHelper.getRecipientDisplayNames` returns "To: Test User" given a prefix "To:". The test failed with "To:Test User" (missing space) using the original code.

I fixed the issue by changing the implementation to initialize an empty `SpannableStringBuilder` and then appending the prefix, space, and recipients sequentially. This ensures the space is correctly appended.

Modified `legacy/core/src/main/java/com/fsck/k9/helper/MessageHelper.kt`:

```kotlin
<<<<
        return SpannableStringBuilder(resourceProvider.contactDisplayNamePrefix()).append(" ").append(recipients)
====
        return SpannableStringBuilder().append(resourceProvider.contactDisplayNamePrefix()).append(" ").append(recipients)
>>>>
```

After this change, the reproduction test passed. I have deleted the temporary test file.

I made the following changes:
- Modified `legacy/core/src/main/java/com/fsck/k9/helper/MessageHelper.kt` to fix the missing space issue.

No other files were modified.

Verified the fix with `legacy/core/src/test/java/com/fsck/k9/helper/MessageHelperGetRecipientDisplayNamesTest.kt` (created and then deleted).

 42→    }

 50→    }

import android.text.SpannableStringBuilder
import app.k9mail.core.android.common.contact.ContactRepository
import app.k9mail.core.android.testing.RobolectricTest
import com.fsck.k9.CoreResourceProvider
import com.fsck.k9.mail.Address
import assertk.assertThat
import assertk.assertions.isEqualTo
import org.junit.Test
import app.k9mail.core.common.mail.EmailAddress
import app.k9mail.core.android.common.contact.Contact

class MessageHelperGetRecipientDisplayNamesTest : RobolectricTest() {

    class FakeResourceProvider : CoreResourceProvider {
        override fun defaultIdentityDescription(): String = ""
        override fun contactDisplayNamePrefix(): String = "To:"
        override fun contactUnknownSender(): String = ""
        override fun contactUnknownRecipient(): String = "Unknown"
        override fun messageHeaderFrom(): String = ""
        override fun messageHeaderTo(): String = ""
        override fun messageHeaderCc(): String = ""
        override fun messageHeaderDate(): String = ""
        override fun messageHeaderSubject(): String = ""
        override fun messageHeaderSeparator(): String = ""
        override fun noSubject(): String = ""
        override fun userAgent(): String = ""
        override fun replyHeader(sender: String): String = ""
        override fun replyHeader(sender: String, sentDate: String): String = ""
        override fun searchUnifiedInboxTitle(): String = ""
        override fun searchUnifiedInboxDetail(): String = ""
        override val iconPushNotification: Int = 0
        override fun pushNotificationText(notificationState: com.fsck.k9.notification.PushNotificationState): String = ""
        override fun pushNotificationInfoText(): String = ""
        override fun pushNotificationGrantAlarmPermissionText(): String = ""
    }

    class FakeContactRepository : ContactRepository {
        override fun getContactFor(emailAddress: EmailAddress): Contact? = null
        override fun hasContactFor(emailAddress: EmailAddress): Boolean = false
    }

    private val resourceProvider = FakeResourceProvider()
    private val contactRepository = FakeContactRepository()
    private val messageHelper = MessageHelper(resourceProvider, contactRepository)

    @Test
    fun getRecipientDisplayNames_shouldIncludeSpaceBetweenPrefixAndAddresses() {
        val addresses = arrayOf(Address("test@example.com", "Test User"))
        
        val result = messageHelper.getRecipientDisplayNames(addresses)
        
        assertThat(result.toString()).isEqualTo("To: Test User")
    }
}
","old_string":"package com.fsck.k9.helper

import android.text.SpannableStringBuilder
import app.k9mail.core.android.common.contact.ContactRepository
import app.k9mail.core.android.testing.RobolectricTest
import com.fsck.k9.CoreResourceProvider
import com.fsck.k9.mail.Address
import assertk.assertThat
import assertk.assertions.isEqualTo
import org.junit.Test
import org.mockito.kotlin.mock
import org.mockito.kotlin.doReturn

class MessageHelperGetRecipientDisplayNamesTest : RobolectricTest() {
    private val resourceProvider: CoreResourceProvider = mock {
        on { contactDisplayNamePrefix() } doReturn "To:"
        on { contactUnknownRecipient() } doReturn "Unknown"
    }
    private val contactRepository: ContactRepository = mock()
    private val messageHelper = MessageHelper(resourceProvider, contactRepository)

    @Test
    fun getRecipientDisplayNames_shouldIncludeSpaceBetweenPrefixAndAddresses() {
        val addresses = arrayOf(Address("test@example.com", "Test User"))
        
        val result = messageHelper.getRecipientDisplayNames(addresses)
        
        assertThat(result.toString()).isEqualTo("To: Test User")
    }
}
